Video.summary: In this episode, define the terms "ancient," "Greek," and "masterpiece."
Video.title: Definitions, Boundaries, and Goals

Video.summary: Consider how the possible oral composition of the Homeric epics might influence our reading.
Video.title: Homer I-Introduction to Epic and Iliad

Image.alt: Homer I-Introduction to Epic and Iliad
Homer I-Introduction to Epic and Iliad
Homer I-Introduction to Epic and Iliad

Video.summary: Here, explore the themes and action of books 2 through 9 of the Iliad.
Video.title: Homer II-Iliad, The Wrath of Achilles

Image.alt: Homer II-Iliad, The Wrath of Achilles
Homer II-Iliad, The Wrath of Achilles
Homer II-Iliad, The Wrath of Achilles

Video.summary: Complete the Iliad, focusing on Achilles's return to battle and its aftermath.
Video.title: Homer III-Iliad, The Return of Achilles

Image.alt: Homer III-Iliad, The Return of Achilles
Homer III-Iliad, The Return of Achilles
Homer III-Iliad, The Return of Achilles

Video.summary: Consider the Odyssey's structure as a traditional nostos, or return story.
Video.title: Homer IV-Odyssey, Introduction and Prelude

Video.summary: Consider the psychic and emotional distance Odysseus travels as he prepares to return home.
Video.title: Homer V-Odyssey, The Adventures

Video.summary: Cover books 13 to 24, following the adventures of Odysseus on Ithaca as he completes his return.
Video.title: Homer VI-Odyssey, Reintegration

Video.summary: Look at two works that share much with the Homeric poems in the form and manner of their composition.
Video.title: Hesiod-Theogony and Works and Days

Video.summary: Explore the influence of Thucydides on modern historians and the value of studying the Peloponnesian War.
Video.title: Thucydides and the Peloponnesian War

Video.summary: The Greek approach to land battles underwent major changes during the Peloponnesian War.
Video.title: The Greek Way of War

Video.summary: Get a balanced view on the impression that Sparta was an authoritarian and soulless society.
Video.title: Sparta - Perceptions and Prejudices

Video.summary: Continue your background exploration of Sparta with a look at the Peloponnesian League.
Video.title: Sparta and Her Allies

Video.summary: Indigenous political aspirations and growing mutual antagonism set the stage for World War I.
Video.grandparentTitle: The United States and the Middle East: 1914 to 9/11
Video.title: A Meeting of Two Worlds

Video.summary: Examine Wilson's efforts to shape the postwar settlement in the Middle East.
Video.grandparentTitle: The United States and the Middle East: 1914 to 9/11
Video.title: Wilson & the Breakup of the Ottoman Empire

Image.alt: Wilson & the Breakup of the Ottoman Empire
Wilson & the Breakup of the Ottoman Empire
Wilson & the Breakup of the Ottoman Empire

Video.summary: Take a look at American interest in the Middle East between the wars.
Video.grandparentTitle: The United States and the Middle East: 1914 to 9/11
Video.title: The Interwar Period

Video.summary: The entry of the United States into World War II altered Americans' conception of the Middle East.
Video.grandparentTitle: The United States and the Middle East: 1914 to 9/11
Video.title: U.S. & the Middle East During World War II

Image.alt: U.S. & the Middle East During World War II
U.S. & the Middle East During World War II
U.S. & the Middle East During World War II

Video.summary: Sparta's rival was Athens, whose most distinctive political institution was democracy.
Video.title: The Athenian Democracy

Video.summary: From 505 to 480 B.C., Athens built the greatest fleet in the Greek world and bolstered its democracy.
Video.title: Athens and the Navy

Video.summary: Sparta and Athens were reluctant allies against Persia, which sought to conquer the Greek homeland.
Video.title: Victory over Persia, 490 - 479 B.C.

Video.summary: As Persian power waned, an alliance between Athens and Sparta led to the foundation of the Delian League.
Video.title: Athens or Sparta - A Question of Leadership

Image.alt: Athens or Sparta - A Question of Leadership
Athens or Sparta - A Question of Leadership
Athens or Sparta - A Question of Leadership

Video.summary: Washington's second term saw parties forming even as Washington remained above the fray.
Video.title: George Washington - American Icon

Video.summary: Thomas Jefferson occupies a special place in American history.
Video.title: Thomas Jefferson - The Pen of Freedom

Image.alt: Thomas Jefferson - The Pen of Freedom
Thomas Jefferson - The Pen of Freedom
Thomas Jefferson - The Pen of Freedom

Video.summary: Jefferson lost the presidency to John Adams in 1796, but he became vice president.
Video.title: Thomas Jefferson - Party Leader

Video.summary: Jefferson's greatest accomplishment as president, the Louisiana Purchase, came in his first term.
Video.title: Thomas Jefferson - Expansionist President

Video.summary: This episode examines the features of late medieval culture in Italy that paved the way for the Renaissance.
Video.grandparentTitle: Great Artists of the Italian Renaissance
Video.title: Italy and the Renaissance

Video.summary: Around 1400, study a European-wide style known as International Gothic flourished in Italy.
Video.grandparentTitle: Great Artists of the Italian Renaissance
Video.title: From Gothic to Renaissance

Video.summary: This episode traces Donatello's work until he moved to Padua in 1443.
Video.grandparentTitle: Great Artists of the Italian Renaissance
Video.title: Donatello and Luca della Robbia

Video.summary: The first of two episodes on Masaccio examines his Pisa Altarpiece.
Video.grandparentTitle: Great Artists of the Italian Renaissance
Video.title: Masaccio

Video.summary: Fra Angelico and Fra Filippo Lippi were the most important painters in Florence after the death of Masaccio.
Video.grandparentTitle: Great Artists of the Italian Renaissance
Video.title: Fra Angelico and Fra Filippo Lippi

Image.alt: Fra Angelico and Fra Filippo Lippi
Fra Angelico and Fra Filippo Lippi
Fra Angelico and Fra Filippo Lippi

Video.summary: Paintings by three contrasting artists: Paolo Uccello, Andrea del Castagno, and Domenico Veneziano.
Video.grandparentTitle: Great Artists of the Italian Renaissance
Video.title: Three Specialists

Video.summary: The Renaissance developed in part through memories of the Roman Empire.
Video.title: Italy - The Cradle of the Renaissance

Image.alt: Italy - The Cradle of the Renaissance
Italy - The Cradle of the Renaissance
Italy - The Cradle of the Renaissance

Video.summary: Learn how the merchant class's need for a different kind of ideology led to the cultivation of humanism.
Video.title: Humanism and the Italian Renaissance
